2606.09389.title: LexRubric: A Rubric-Guided Diagnostic Benchmark for Open-Ended Legal Tasks
2606.09389.paper_summary: As large language models (LLMs) are increasingly applied to real-world legal tasks, evaluating the reliability of their open-ended legal responses has become essential. These tasks require context-sensitive answers and allow little room for error, motivating fine-grained and diagnostic evaluation that can identify specific sources of response quality failures. We introduce LexRubric, a rubric-based benchmark for evaluating open-ended Chinese legal tasks. LexRubric contains 649 instances from legal consultation and judicial examination, which reflect both everyday legal needs and professional legal reasoning and cover 14 legal scenarios. It further includes 12,337 expert-written atomic scoring criteria organized under a unified six-dimensional framework, enabling accurate evaluation and diagnostic analysis across tasks and evaluation dimensions. To validate the reliability of the evaluation, we test multiple judge models and compare model-based judgments with human judgments. We further evaluate 18 recent general and legal-domain LLMs on LexRubric. Results show that different models exhibit distinct capability profiles, and that open-ended legal question remains challenging for current LLMs. Data is available at: https://github.com/foggpoy/LexRubric.
2606.09389.paper_summary_zh: 隨著大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地應用於現實世界的法律任務，評估它們的開放式法律回應的可靠性變得至關重要。這些任務需要上下文敏感的答案，並且容錯空間極小，促使進行細緻且診斷性的評估，以識別回應質量失敗的具體來源。我們介紹了LexRubric，一個基於評分標準的基準，用於評估開放式中文法律任務。LexRubric包含649個來自法律諮詢和司法考試的實例，這些實例反映了日常法律需求和專業法律推理，涵蓋了14個法律場景。它還包括12,337個專家撰寫的原子評分標準，這些標準在統一的六維框架下組織，能夠在任務和評估維度之間進行準確的評估和診斷分析。為了驗證評估的可靠性，我們測試了多個評審模型，並將基於模型的判斷與人類判斷進行比較。我們還在LexRubric上評估了18個最近的通用和法律領域LLMs。結果顯示，不同模型展現出不同的能力特徵，而開放式法律問題對當前的LLMs來說仍然具有挑戰性。數據可在以下網址獲得：https://github.com/foggpoy/LexRubric。
2606.09389.author: Yifan Chen et.al.
2606.09389.authors: Yifan Chen, Haitao Li, Yiran Hu, Kaisong Song, Jun Lin, Yueyue Wu, Qingyao Ai, Min Zhang, Yiqun Liu
